basicAuthUser.description: The username of the data source account

details.description: Leave blank to ignore. Format: { "message": "your message", "verboseMessage":"detailed message"}

message.description: custom health check message. Leave blank for empty message

skipBackend.description: allow you to skip backend call for health check

customHealthCheckEnabled.description: allow you to have custom health check messages

serverName.description: A Servername is used to verify the hostname on the returned certificate

tlsAuth.description: Validate using TLS client authentication, in which the server authenticates the client

tlsAuthWithCACert.description: Add your own Certificate Authority (CA) certificate on top of one generated by the certificate authorities for additional security measures

tlsSkipVerify.description: Skipping TLS certificate validation is not recommended unless absolutely necessary or for testing

secureValues.description: The password of the data source account
The client certificate can be generated from a Certificate Authority or be self-signed
The client key can be generated from a Certificate Authority or be self-signed

examples.description: The defaults a new datasource starts with: no authentication, no TLS overrides, no custom health check. root.url can be blank — the Mock plugin never dials it.

basicAuth.description: Authenticate the SDK HTTP client with HTTP Basic. Both basicAuth and basicAuthUser live at the datasource root; only basicAuthPassword is secret.

customHealthCheckError.description: Use the CustomHealthCheck override to force a failing CheckHealth response for testing. status=2 maps to backend.HealthStatusError; details is passed through as jsonDetails on the CheckHealth response.

customHealthCheckSkipBackend.description: With skipBackend=true, the frontend testDatasource() short-circuits and never calls the backend (src/datasource.ts:32-58). The response is synthesised entirely from jsonData.customHealthCheck. status=1 maps to a success response on the UI side.

noAuth.description: A Mock datasource with a plausible URL and no HTTP-level auth. The Mock backend does not dial the URL; it is only relevant when downstream tools proxy through the SDK's HTTP client.

oauthForward.description: Forward the signed-in user's upstream OAuth identity through the SDK HTTP client. The editor writes only jsonData.oauthPassThru; there is no accompanying secret.

tlsMutualAuth.description: SDK HTTP client using mTLS. jsonData.tlsAuth=true triggers the SDK to build a client-authenticated transport using serverName plus the PEM-encoded client cert and key in secureJsonData.

secureJsonData.tlsClientKey:
-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
...
-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----

tlsSelfSignedCA.description: SDK HTTP client verifying the upstream against a private CA. jsonData.tlsAuthWithCACert=true tells the SDK to verify the server certificate against the PEM-encoded CA in secureJsonData.tlsCACert.
